title: Nicole Graham
text:
Hello,
I am a graduate student in the Masters of Geomatics for Environmental Management in the Faculty of Forestry and Environmental Stewardship at the University of British Columbia. I have an undergraduate degree in Oceanography and Biology and am interested in the applications of geomatics for aquatic systems and wildlife ecology.
My previous experience includes data extraction and management for marine contaminants in BC, helping mitigate effects of hydroelectric dams on terrestrial and aquatic systems and helping build the understanding of Pacific salmon ecology through analyzing samples of their diet.
Through my education and experiences, I have gained skills in analyzing and visualizing data using programming languages such as R, Python, SQL and GIS platforms like QGIS and ArcGIS Pro. I have also utilized critical thinking skills, technical writing, time management and collaboration within interdisciplinary teams.

title: Resume
text:
Education
Master of Geomatics for Environmental Management
University of British Columbia
Jul 2025 - Apr 2026
Bachelor of Science Combined Major in Oceanography and Biology
University of British Columbia
Sept 2017 - Apr 2023


Work Experience
Research Study Assistant | SolveFSHD
Nov 2023 - Sept 2025

Developed a clinical database for early-onset Facioscapulohumeral Dystrophy (FSHD) cases, supporting a retrospective natural history study under the guidance of the Executive Director.
Collaborated with FSHD advocates and leadership to define essential data parameters and key information.
Presented analytical results and provided clear, concise summaries and presentations.

Research Assistant | Pacific Salmon Foundation
Oct 2024 - Aug 2025

Support the maintenance of the Contaminant Atlas, a geospatial database of environmental samples containing contaminants harmful to Pacific salmon.
Extract and organize marine contaminant data from historical reports to enhance database accuracy.
Collaborate with team members to develop automated processes for efficient data extraction.

Fisheries/Environmental Co-op Student | BC Hydro
Sept - Dec 2022

Acquired in-depth knowledge of hydroelectric dams and their environmental, socio-economic, and Indigenous community impacts across British Columbia.
Contributed to regulatory compliance initiatives, including monitoring programs aimed at mitigating pollution and protecting land and aquatic ecosystems.
Gained familiarity with provincial and federal environmental standards and regulations, including the Fisheries Act and Contaminated Sites Regulation.
Supported the preparation and coordination of fish entrainment studies at various BC Hydro facilities.

Research/Laboratory Co-op Student | Pelagic Ecosystems Lab at UBC
Jan - Aug 2021

Contributed to research on Pacific salmon and zooplankton ecology in the North Pacific Ocean.
Prepared and processed salmon and zooplankton samples for analysis and organized/presented data from literature on pelagic ecosystems.
Independently operated lab equipment, adhering to safety protocols and maintaining cleanliness standards.



Volunteer Experience
Beaty Biodiversity Museum at UBC
Sept 2024 - Apr 2025

Cataloged specimens for the Bryophytes, Lichens and Fungi collection.

Cataloged books for the Marine Invertebrate collection.

BCSPCA
May 2019 - Jan 2023

Improved wildlife photo database and retrieval process by including species identification and descriptions.

Supported annual fundraising calender contest by managing phtot submission and responding to inquiries.

title: Projects and Publications
text:
Below are projects I have undertaken or been a part of through my work experience and education.

Projects:
Contaminant Atlas

The Contaminant Atlas is a geospatial database run by the Pacific Salmon Foundation - It contains information on marine contaminant samples that are harmful to Pacific salmon.
I maintained this database through extracting and updating existing information on historical reports.

Undergraduate Directed Studies:
Effects of microplastics on Scenedesmus obliqqus aggregation

I designed and performed an experiment to investigate phytoplankton aggregation in response to microplastic exposure over time.
I utilized the Flowcam to capture images of phytoplankton.




Images of Scenedesmus obliqqus




Publications:
Ogushi, R., Sun, E., Campbell, L. R. E., Chandrakumar, F. B., Fort, R., Graham, N., Grebert, J., Grewal, O., Habib, I., Hamamoto, S. C., Ho, K., Huang, Y., Kim, A., Manocha, N. K., Pandher, K., Radakovich, E., Raghuraman, S., Read, T., Roh, S. T., … Tseng, M. (2024). Lepidoptera species richness and community composition in urban street trees. Canadian Journal of Zoology, 102(6), 556–564. https://doi.org/10.1139/cjz-2023-0150

title: Content & Deliverables
text:
Project Deliverable 1
This is a sample page where you can archive project deliverables.

title: MGEM
text:
MGEM Capstone project (FCOR 599):

in progress

Investigating the Intersection of Modeled and Observed Pacific Salmon Streams with Conserved and Protected Areas in British Columbia.
My capstone project uses stream layers from the GitHub repository bcfishpass which represent modeled and observed spawning and rearing streams and migration paths for Pacific salmon species across BC. I am intersecting these streams with conserved and protected layers so see which salmon species and regions across BC have these modeled and observed streams. This information will allow conservationists to determine which Pacific salmon species could have access to conserved and protected areas. This will also allow for the potential to introduce Pacific salmon to these areas allowing for increased habitat connectivity and improve the stability of these populations in BC.



Map and chart of the conserved and protected areas in BC





Map of spawning and rearing streams from bcfishpass




Code Snippets
Below is a SQL code snippet that I am using to create the intersections of streams and conserved and protected areas.

SQL


CREATE TABLE streams_in_conserved AS
SELECT
    -- bcfishpass stream data
    s.fid,
    s.length_metre,
    s.spawning_ch,
    s.spawning_cm,
    s.spawning_co,
    s.spawning_pk,
    s.spawning_sk,
    s.spawning_st,
    s.rearing_ch,
    s.rearing_sk,
    s.rearing_st,
    s.rearing_co,
    s.watershed_group_code,

    -- conserved and protected areas (all columns, already preprocessed)
    c.objectid AS conserved_id,
      c.harvest_restrictions_id,
    c.land_designation_name,
    c.land_designation_type_rank,
      c.designation,
    c.harvest_restriction_class_rank,
    c.harvest_restriction_class_name,
    c.og_restriction,
    c.mine_restriction,
    c.shape_area,
    c.shape_length,
    c.overall_desig

FROM bcfishpass_stream s
JOIN conserved_areas_processed c
  ON s.geom && c.SHAPE     
 AND ST_Intersects(s.geom, c.SHAPE);





Assignments
Through the various assignments in MGEM, I have gained hands-on experience in addressing environmental management issues using GIS, remote sensing, statistics and landscape ecology. Below are some examples of assignments that highlight my learning.
GEM 520/521:



Time-Series Analysis and land cover change detection using R. Through this assignment, I was able to calculate median NDVI over time, apply a change detection algorithm for two AOI and determine net changes in forest are. This figure shows the changes in land cover between two time periods





Worked with LiDAR points clouds and Canopy height models to detected individual trees. This figure shows individual tree segmentation from LiDAR point clouds using R


GEM 510/511:



Performed a least cost path analysis on Grizzly Bears in Alberta using vectors and rasters using OSGeo4W shell, QGIS and ArcGISPro





Utilized hydrology, network analyst and linear referencing tools in ArcGIS Pro to model a salmon stream network. This figure shows the reachable and not reachable streams for the Georgia Strait Chinook conservation unit inlcuing the presence of dams

text: Sample leaflet - for detailed leaflet instructions, visit the FCOR 599 workshop archive page here.

CREATE TABLE streams_in_conserved AS
SELECT
    -- bcfishpass stream data
    s.fid,
    s.length_metre,
    s.spawning_ch,
    s.spawning_cm,
    s.spawning_co,
    s.spawning_pk,
    s.spawning_sk,
    s.spawning_st,
    s.rearing_ch,
    s.rearing_sk,
    s.rearing_st,
    s.rearing_co,
    s.watershed_group_code,

    -- conserved and protected areas (all columns, already preprocessed)
    c.objectid AS conserved_id,
      c.harvest_restrictions_id,
    c.land_designation_name,
    c.land_designation_type_rank,
      c.designation,
    c.harvest_restriction_class_rank,
    c.harvest_restriction_class_name,
    c.og_restriction,
    c.mine_restriction,
    c.shape_area,
    c.shape_length,
    c.overall_desig

FROM bcfishpass_stream s
JOIN conserved_areas_processed c
  ON s.geom && c.SHAPE     
 AND ST_Intersects(s.geom, c.SHAPE);
